proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_012 is positively associated with pfs_months across the cohort.
The ordinal variable feature_005 (values 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict shorter PFS.
The binary variable feature_006 is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1) compared to absent (=0).
The binary variable feature_001 is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1).
The binary variable feature_013 is associated with longer pfs_months when present (=1).
The binary variable feature_029 is associated with longer pfs_months when present (=1).
The binary variable feature_028 is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1).
The binary variable feature_018 is associated with shorter pfs_months when present (=1).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.spearmanr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.726, p<1e-300. feature_012 (range 30-90, mean 65) is the strongest single predictor of PFS; mean PFS rises from 1.70 in lowest decile to 7.84 in highest decile.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway(df.loc[df['feature_005']==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_005']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_005']==2,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_005 level: 5.64 (n=17592), 4.44 (n=24971), 3.29 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA p<<1e-300. Difference top-vs-bottom = -2.35 months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 (n=14969) mean PFS 3.62 vs feature_006=0 (n=35031) mean 5.16. Difference -1.54 months, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=1 (n=5090) mean PFS 3.75 vs feature_001=0 (n=44910) mean 4.80. Difference -1.05 months, p=1.5e-178.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 (n=17428) mean PFS 5.39 vs feature_013=0 (n=32572) mean 4.29. Difference +1.10 months, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind by feature_029
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=1 (n=34886) mean PFS 4.86 vs feature_029=0 (n=15114) mean 4.30. Difference +0.57 months, p=3.7e-143.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=1 (n=17365) mean PFS 4.33 vs feature_028=0 (n=32635) mean 4.89. Difference -0.56 months, p=6.1e-144.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind by feature_018
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=1 (n=9060) mean PFS 4.32 vs feature_018=0 (n=40940) mean 4.78. Difference -0.46 months, p=6.1e-70.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, feature_012 remains an independent positive predictor of pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, several of the apparent univariate associations (e.g., feature_023, feature_016) lose significance, suggesting they were confounded.
feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100) is independently negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_024 (continuous, 1.5-5.5) is independently positively associated with pfs_months after adjustment.
feature_004 (continuous, 0-25) is independently negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: statsmodels.api.OLS(y, sm.add_constant(X_all_features)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.79. Independently significant (|t|>3): feature_012 +0.176/unit, feature_005 -1.17/level, feature_006 -1.55, feature_001 -0.98, feature_029 +0.53, feature_018 -0.47, feature_028 -0.58, feature_033 -0.019/unit, feature_024 +0.476/unit, feature_004 -0.077/unit, feature_013 +1.10, feature_031 -5e-4/unit. feature_023 (+0.014, p=0.32) and feature_016 (+0.002, p=0.86) lose significance after adjustment, suggesting their univariate signals were entirely confounded by feature_013/feature_029.

analyses.code: OLS coefficient on feature_033
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 adjusted coefficient = -0.0189 per unit (t=-45, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS coefficient on feature_024
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 adjusted coefficient = +0.476 per unit (t=47, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS coefficient on feature_004
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 adjusted coefficient = -0.077 per unit (t=-58, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 is primarily prognostic rather than predictive of treatment effect: its association with pfs_months is similar in patients with feature_029=0 and feature_029=1.
feature_005 (ordinal performance-status-like) does not modify the magnitude of the feature_029 treatment effect.

analyses.code: spearmanr separately in feature_029=0 vs feature_029=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 rho with PFS = +0.814 (feature_029=0) vs +0.693 (feature_029=1); both highly significant; difference small. Pattern indicates feature_012 is mainly prognostic.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_029 by feature_005 within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_029 effect: +2.86 (feature_005=0), +3.00 (feature_005=1), +2.77 (feature_005=2). Effect magnitudes very similar; feature_005 does not appreciably modify treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all pairs of binary features, feature_029 x feature_013 shows the strongest interaction on pfs_months: the effect of feature_029 is much larger when feature_013=1.
feature_018 x feature_013 shows a strong negative interaction: feature_018 nullifies the favorable effect of feature_013.
feature_023 x feature_013 shows a strong positive interaction: feature_023 only adds benefit when feature_013=1.

analyses.code: for each pair (a,b): OLS pfs ~ a + b + a*b
analyses.result_summary: Top binary x binary interactions on PFS (interaction term): feature_029 x feature_013 +1.59 (p=9e-229); feature_018 x feature_013 -1.36 (p=2e-118); feature_023 x feature_013 +1.09 (p=3e-117); feature_029 x feature_028 -0.80 (p=1e-56); feature_018 x feature_028 +0.70 (p=4e-31); feature_023 x feature_028 -0.56 (p=1e-30).

analyses.code: ttest of feature_029 stratified by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0: feature_029 effect = +0.011 (p=0.68, NS). Among feature_013=1: feature_029 effect = +1.605 (p<<0.001). Treatment effect is fully concentrated in feature_013=1.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_018 stratified by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0: feature_018 effect = +0.015 (p=0.64). Among feature_013=1: feature_018 effect = -1.349 (p=3e-180). feature_018 essentially abolishes the feature_013 benefit.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_023 stratified by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0: feature_023 effect = -0.020 (NS). Among feature_013=1: feature_023 effect = +1.072 (p=5e-134).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 is a negative effect modifier of feature_029: when feature_028=1, the feature_029 benefit is abolished.
feature_028 is a negative effect modifier of feature_023: when feature_028=1, the feature_023 benefit is abolished.
feature_018 is a negative effect modifier of feature_023: when feature_018=1, the feature_023 benefit is abolished.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_029 stratified by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_028=0: feature_029 effect = +0.845 (p=2e-193). Among feature_028=1: feature_029 effect = +0.045 (p=0.20, NS). feature_028 fully blocks feature_029 benefit.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_023 stratified by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_028=0: feature_023 effect = +0.554 (p=7e-81). Among feature_028=1: feature_023 effect = -0.003 (p=0.93, NS).

analyses.code: ttest of feature_023 stratified by feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_018=0: feature_023 effect = +0.436 (p=5e-67). Among feature_018=1: feature_023 effect = +0.018 (p=0.69, NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup test: the favorable effect of feature_029 on pfs_months is restricted to the joint subgroup feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0; in every other 3-way cell defined by these three biomarkers, the feature_029 effect is null.
The same joint biomarker subgroup (feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0) defines the patients in whom feature_023 has a favorable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each (v13,v28,v18) cell: ttest of feature_029
for each (v13,v28,v18) cell: ttest of feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 effect across 8 cells: (013=1,028=0,018=0): +1.97 (p=4e-199); all other 7 cells |effect|<0.1 and NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup (feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0), feature_029 and feature_023 are the only binary features with significant effects on pfs_months apart from the negative biomarkers feature_006 and feature_001.
Within the responsive subgroup, feature_006 and feature_001 retain their negative prognostic effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each binary feature in subgroup: ttest
analyses.result_summary: Within the responsive subgroup (n=9326), only four binary features have p<0.01 association with PFS: feature_029 (+2.94, p=0), feature_023 (+1.97, p=4e-199), feature_006 (-1.52, p=2e-98), feature_001 (-1.12, p=4e-22). All other binary features have |eff|<0.3 and p>0.05.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_006 and feature_001 in responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: -1.52 months (p=2e-98); feature_001: -1.12 months (p=4e-22). Effect magnitudes nearly identical to those in the whole cohort, indicating these are subgroup-independent prognostic markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 has very limited additional benefit on top of feature_029: most of its apparent effect within the responsive subgroup is driven by correlation with feature_029 use.

analyses.code: 2x2 means in responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup (n=9326): mean PFS by (feature_029, feature_023) = (0,0) 4.255 (n=2339), (0,1) 4.343 (n=416), (1,0) 7.023 (n=976), (1,1) 7.241 (n=5595). feature_023 marginal benefit on top of feature_029 = +0.22 months (small but statistically present).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_033 modifies the feature_029 treatment effect: as feature_033 increases, the magnitude of feature_029 benefit decreases (negative interaction).
There is no continuous feature x feature_029 interaction of comparable magnitude to feature_033 in the responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: for each continuous feature: OLS pfs ~ feature_029 + cont + feature_029*cont within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_033 shows a significant continuous-by-treatment interaction: int_eff = -0.133 per unit feature_033 (p=1e-160). All other continuous features have interaction p > 0.01 in the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_029 treatment effect within the responsive subgroup follows a threshold pattern in feature_033: in patients with feature_033 below ~15 the treatment yields ~+5 months PFS, but above ~15 the effect collapses to essentially zero.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_029 by feature_033 bin within responsive subgroup

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full subgroup that defines patients responsive to feature_029 on pfs_months is feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= 15. In this subgroup the feature_029 effect is approximately +4.6 months; in any patient violating any one of these four conditions the effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_029 in maximal responsive subgroup (4 conditions)
analyses.result_summary: Maximal responsive subgroup n=5756 (11.5% of cohort). Mean PFS feature_029=1: 8.94 (n=4098); feature_029=0: 4.31 (n=1658). Effect = +4.62 months (p=0). Sensitivity to feature_033 cutoff: cut<=10 +5.02; cut<=12 +4.94; cut<=14 +4.93; cut<=15 +4.62; cut<=16 +4.39 -- monotonic dilution as cutoff loosens.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The same maximal subgroup (feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= 15) defines the patients in whom feature_023 has a favorable effect on pfs_months; in this subgroup, feature_023 effect approaches +3 months.
Within the maximal responsive subgroup, when feature_029 is held fixed, feature_023 provides only a small marginal benefit (~+0.3 months) over feature_029 alone.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_023 in maximal responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 effect in maximal subgroup (n=5756): +3.07 months (p=8e-270, m1=8.69, m0=4.42). Most of this effect is collinear with feature_029.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_023 by feature_029 in maximal subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within maximal subgroup, feature_029=1 stratum (n=4098): feature_023 marginal effect = +0.29 months (p=0.010). feature_029=0 stratum (n=1658): feature_023 effect = +0.15 months (p=0.28, NS). Feature_023 is essentially an add-on to feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_028=1 stratum (likely a resistance biomarker), no binary feature provides a positive treatment-like effect on pfs_months: only the prognostic markers feature_006 and feature_001 are significantly associated.
Within the feature_018=1 stratum, no binary feature provides a positive treatment-like effect on pfs_months.
Within the feature_013=0 stratum (likely a negative biomarker for the treatment), no binary feature provides a positive treatment-like effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each binary feature in feature_028=1: ttest
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_028=1 (n=17365): only feature_006 (-1.53, p=0) and feature_001 (-1.01, p=3e-84) reach p<0.01; no positive (treatment-like) effects.

analyses.code: for each binary feature in feature_018=1: ttest
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_018=1 (n=9060): only feature_006 (-1.58, p=4e-237) and feature_001 (-0.99, p=3e-40) reach p<0.01; no positive effects.

analyses.code: for each binary feature in feature_013=0: ttest
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0 (n=32572): only feature_006 (-1.54, p=0) and feature_001 (-1.03, p=4e-155) reach p<0.01; no treatment-like effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 is a negative prognostic marker whose effect magnitude is similar across treatment status and across biomarker subgroups (uniform main effect).
feature_006 does not modify the magnitude of the feature_029 treatment effect within the maximal responsive subgroup.
feature_001 does not modify the magnitude of the feature_029 treatment effect within the maximal responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_006 across many subgroups
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 negative effect is observed in feature_028=1 (-1.53), feature_018=1 (-1.58), feature_013=0 (-1.54), and maximal responsive subgroup (-1.46). Magnitudes nearly identical: feature_006 acts as a uniform prognostic marker.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_029 by feature_006 in maximal responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_029 effect: +4.77 in feature_006=0 vs +4.36 in feature_006=1. Magnitudes very similar; feature_006 has no meaningful interaction with feature_029.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_029 by feature_001 in maximal responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_029 effect: +4.63 in feature_001=0 vs +4.62 in feature_001=1. Identical magnitudes; feature_001 has no interaction with feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (binary, prevalence ~45%) has no association with pfs_months.
feature_030 (binary, prevalence ~50%) has no association with pfs_months.
feature_019 (binary, prevalence ~60%) has no association with pfs_months.
feature_009 (binary, rare, prevalence ~2.5%) has no significant association with pfs_months.
feature_026 (binary, rare, prevalence ~2.4%) has no significant association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 mean PFS difference = -0.013, p=0.56. No association.

analyses.code: ttest by feature_030
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 mean PFS difference = -0.024, p=0.29. No association.

analyses.code: ttest by feature_019
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 mean PFS difference = -0.023, p=0.31. No association.

analyses.code: ttest by feature_009
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 mean PFS difference = -0.024, p=0.72. No association.

analyses.code: ttest by feature_026
analyses.result_summary: feature_026 mean PFS difference = -0.127, p=0.08. No significant association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most continuous lab-like features (feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_014, feature_017, feature_021, feature_025, feature_027, feature_034, feature_035, feature_036, feature_037) have no clinically meaningful association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: spearmanr each continuous feature with PFS
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho with PFS: feature_002 -0.001 (p=0.82); feature_007 +0.009 (p=0.04); feature_010 -0.013 (p=0.004); feature_014 -0.003 (p=0.58); feature_017 -0.005 (p=0.28); feature_021 +0.003 (p=0.51); feature_025 -0.004 (p=0.39); feature_027 -0.011 (p=0.02); feature_034 -0.000 (p=0.93); feature_035 -0.000 (p=0.98); feature_036 -0.002 (p=0.63); feature_037 -0.009 (p=0.05). All effect sizes <|0.02| and clinically negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (continuous, range 0-25) has a small but real negative association with pfs_months that persists in multivariable adjustment but does not modify treatment effects.
feature_024 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) has a positive association with pfs_months independent of treatment.

analyses.code: spearmanr in whole cohort and in responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Overall rho = -0.116 (p=5e-149). Within responsive subgroup rho = -0.055 (p=3e-5). Effect persists but smaller in subgroup; consistent with mild prognostic role.

analyses.code: spearmanr in whole cohort and responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Overall rho = +0.102 (p=1e-115). Within responsive subgroup rho = +0.065 (p=7e-7). Mild positive prognostic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 (binary, ~37% prevalence) has only a marginal univariate association with pfs_months, no independent effect after adjustment, and is not a treatment-effect modifier of clinical magnitude in the maximal responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: ttest of feature_016 univariate, multivariable, and within maximal subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: +0.114 (p=1e-6). Multivariable adjusted: +0.002 (p=0.86, NS). In maximal responsive subgroup: +0.116 (p=0.17, NS). feature_016 has no clinically meaningful effect on PFS once feature_013/feature_029 are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (ordinal performance-status-like marker) has a strong negative dose-response association with pfs_months that is independent of treatment status and biomarker subgroups.

analyses.code: ANOVA of feature_005 levels and OLS adjusted coefficient
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 5.64 (level 0) -> 4.44 (level 1) -> 3.29 (level 2). Linear adjusted coefficient = -1.17 per level (p<<1e-300). Effect dose-monotonic and independent of feature_029 effect (similar within all responsive-subgroup strata: +2.86, +3.00, +2.77).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 acts almost purely as a prognostic factor: its effect on pfs_months does not depend strongly on whether feature_029 is administered, on feature_013 status, on feature_028, or on feature_018.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: OLS pfs ~ feature_012 + binary + feature_012*binary
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 x binary interaction terms are all very small (|int| < 0.012 per unit feature_012); only modest evidence of mild interaction with feature_006 (-0.011, p=3e-12) and feature_001 (-0.008, p=4e-3). Overall feature_012 acts as a strong prognostic marker (per-unit slope ~+0.18 months) with little treatment-effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_029: a recursive subgroup decomposition over (feature_013, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033 binned) shows treatment effect concentrated entirely in the single multi-condition subgroup feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= 15.

Treatment-effect heterogeneity search for feature_023 yields the same multi-condition subgroup as for feature_029, with feature_023 acting essentially as an adjunct to feature_029 within that subgroup.

analyses.code: exhaustive 4-way subgrouping with feature_033 cuts and ttest in each cell
analyses.result_summary: Across the 16 cells defined by (feature_013, feature_028, feature_018) x (feature_033<=15 vs >15), feature_029 effect is significant only in the (1,0,0,<=15) cell where it averages +4.62 months. In every other cell |effect|<0.3 and p>0.05.

analyses.code: same exhaustive 4-way subgrouping for feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 effect significant only in (1,0,0,<=15) cell at +3.07 months (most of which is collinear with feature_029). Marginal effect of feature_023 over feature_029 alone in this cell ~+0.3 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the maximal responsive subgroup (i.e., violating any of feature_013=1 / feature_028=0 / feature_018=0 / feature_033<=15), no binary or continuous feature behaves as a beneficial treatment for pfs_months.

analyses.code: for each feature, test effect within complement of maximal subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Outside the maximal responsive subgroup (n=44244), every feature has |effect on PFS|<0.3 except prognostic markers (feature_006 ~ -1.5, feature_001 ~ -1.0, feature_005 monotone, feature_012 strong positive). No feature shows a treatment-like positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The mean pfs_months in the maximal responsive subgroup (~7.6 months, n=5756) is substantially higher than in the rest of the cohort (~4.3 months, n=44244), confirming the subgroup carries a strong joint prognostic and treatment-response signature.

analyses.code: compare means inside vs outside maximal subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS inside maximal subgroup (n=5756) = 7.605. Mean PFS outside (n=44244) = 4.314. Difference +3.29 months (p<<1e-300, Welch t-test).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for pfs_months: feature_029 yields a substantial positive effect on pfs_months (~+4.6 months) only in patients with feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 <= 15. Patients failing any single one of these four conditions derive essentially no benefit (effect ~ 0).

Final treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for feature_023: in the same maximal subgroup, feature_023 produces a small positive marginal effect (~+0.3 months) over and above feature_029. Outside that subgroup it has no benefit.

analyses.code: final ttest in maximal subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Maximal responsive subgroup (n=5756, 11.5% of cohort): feature_029 effect = +4.62 months PFS (p=0). Outside subgroup (n=44244): feature_029 effect = +0.05 months (p=0.18). All four conditions are necessary: removing feature_013=1 -> 0 effect; removing feature_028=0 -> 0 effect; removing feature_018=0 -> 0 effect; removing feature_033<=15 -> effect drops to ~0.2.

analyses.code: marginal ttest of feature_023 within maximal subgroup conditional on feature_029=1
analyses.result_summary: In maximal subgroup with feature_029=1: feature_023 marginal effect = +0.29 months (p=0.010). In same subgroup with feature_029=0: feature_023 effect = +0.15 months (p=0.28).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary: pfs_months is driven by (a) a large prognostic component dominated by feature_012 (positive), feature_005 (negative dose), feature_006 (negative), feature_001 (negative), feature_004 (negative), feature_024 (positive) and (b) a treatment-response component restricted to the joint subgroup feature_013=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<=15 in which feature_029 (and adjunct feature_023) deliver a ~+4.6 month PFS benefit.

analyses.code: synthesis -- no new test; restates the validated pattern
analyses.result_summary: Restated synthesis of the supported hypotheses; no new test. Together the prognostic and treatment-response components account for ~79% of the variance in pfs_months.
